summary: Monstrous Alligator suite, soaring Eleven and Dark Star brilliance
blurb: The show delivers a monster Alligator > Caution marathon and an electrifying Eleven that locks the band into high gear. Jerry’s guitar soars on Lovelight and The Other One, while Phil’s bass drives the complex time‑signatures.
review: February 28, 1969 at Fillmore West remains one of the Dead’s most legendary marathons. A three‑set assault that begins with a Pigpen‑charged first set, erupts into an almost mythic second‑set suite (Cryptical Envelopment → The Other One → Dark Star → Saint Stephen → The Eleven) and culminates in the monumental Alligator > Caution > Feedback > And We Bid You Good Night sequence. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s inspired lead—especially his tone on “Lovelight” and relentless drive through “The Other One”—and Phil’s bass, described as “insane” during the Eleven. The twin‑drum attack of Bill and Mickey is tight and powerful, propelling the extended jams despite a few minor speed/pitch quirks in the drum segment. With crystal‑clear soundboard mixes (the multi‑track version offering unparalleled instrument separation), this night captures the Dead at their most adventurous and cohesive.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Alligator suite, Eleven, Dark Star and Lovelight. No negative comments appear across analyses; the consistent high praise for multiple key moments warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Multi‑track soundboard mix provides crystal‑clear instrument separation and captures the marathon suite with exceptional detail, despite minor pitch wobble that has been corrected in newer releases.

key_highlights: Alligator > Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) > Feedback > And We Bid You Good Night marathon
The Eleven delivers a thunderous, time‑warp performance
Dark Star segues into Saint Stephen and The Eleven with seamless psychedelic flow
Jerry’s tone on Lovelight is described as perfect and mind‑blowing
song_highlights: And We Bid You Good Night

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is prominent and inspired throughout, with a perfect tone on Lovelight and mind‑blowing leads on The Other One and the Eleven.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh delivers an insanely powerful bass line, especially locking in during Stephen and the Eleven, driving the complex rhythmic passages.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming pairs tightly with Bill, providing a powerful backbone; the drum segment within the Alligator suite is highlighted despite minor speed/pitch quirks.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s interplay with Mickey is praised as tight and energetic, anchoring the extended jams.

must_listen_sequences: And We Bid You Good Night
